mnemonics: My Only Neat Alphabet Replaces Each Letter - M.O.N.A.R.E.L. (Monoalphabetic Cipher)
keyPoints: A monoalphabetic cipher uses a fixed substitution to replace every letter in the plaintext with a corresponding ciphertext letter.
The cipher relies on a single, consistent alphabet mapping for the entire encryption process (e.g., A->X, B->M, C->O for all messages).
It is vulnerable to frequency analysis attacks because it preserves the frequency patterns of the original language.
commonMistakes: Confusing it with a polyalphabetic cipher (like the Vigenère cipher), which uses multiple substitution alphabets.
Assuming it is secure for modern encryption due to its simplicity and high vulnerability to cryptanalysis.
